verdicts.note: The diff adds an explicit engine-vs-agent trust model, forbids agent-text shell substitution, constrains shell commands to human-committed YAML, and path-contains P7 proposal application to a versioned-asset allowlist.
P1 contract tests are now constrained to tool-less/read-only CLI smoke runs, with write-flag coverage isolated to disposable fixture repos.
The plan now records per-step base SHAs, detects dirty interrupted worktrees, defines park/reset handling, and adds crash tests for both post-edit/pre-manifest and mid-commit failures.

The judge operational model now distinguishes deny from unreachable, keeps unattended fail-closed behavior, and gives interactive bootstrap sessions an ask-with-warning fallback plus health/restart handling.
A minimal redacting writer is moved into P1 and is required for manual transcripts, event streams, and the P2 judge audit log before full P4 configurability exists.
P4 now includes a minimal bootstrap pipeline and prompt stubs sufficient for the P5-P7 loop, while leaving polished standard assets for P5.

The red-team suite is expanded beyond shell commands to cover write/edit escapes, symlinks, credential reads, non-Bash operations, package-manager variants, quoting evasions, and the workspace-write backstop.
decline accepted; given v1 has no third-party plugin model and installed entry-point code already shares the Python environment trust boundary, deferring plugin allowlisting is defensible.
The triage exit criteria now require a severity-stratified corpus, per-severity confusion matrix, confidence/escalation behavior, and zero unescalated blocking-severity misses.
Rollback now refuses dirty or diverged branches and records both a backup ref and manifest snapshot before rewind.
The entry contract now defines an enforceable non-stub PRD check while documenting authorship as procedural.
decline accepted; keeping default log inclusion while relying on default redaction and optional exclusion is a defensible product choice rather than a correctness gap.
The stale PRD text was corrected to preserve review-fix commits as first-class history with squash-merge only optional at PR time.

summary: All reviewed findings are resolved or have defensible accepted declines; the scoped diff introduces no new findings.
